titleStmt.title: A Transcription of John in Codex Aureus
VL15
The New Testament
The Gospel according to John
john
titleStmt.funder: The Arts and Humanities Research Council

sponsor.name: The Institute for Textual Scholarship and Electronic Editing

publisher.name: The Institute for Textual Scholarship and Electronic Editing
University of Birmingham

availability.p: Licensed under a Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International License (CC BY 4.0): available for re-use provided attribution is made to the original creators (the IGNTP and any named participants) and an indication is given of any changes made. See further .

msItem.title: Quattuor Euangelia
Four Gospels in order Mt Mc Lc Jo

decoDesc.p: Gold, black and red ink. Most pages are arranged in a decorative way, with the use of different coloured ink to pick out a design, or text squeezed into predetermined shapes.

surrogates.bibl: Black and white photographs in ITSEE.

projectDesc.p: This transcription was made by the Verbum Project as part of an online and printed edition of the Old Latin versions of John between 2003 and 2010.

editorialDecl.p: This transcription covers ff. 149r-191v. The gospel is preceded by capitula (Type I). Running titles are only present on certain purple pages and were apparently added later (as were some of the chapter numbers). At the bottom of most columns, the conclusion of the word, or a group of words, are written in smaller font and centre- or right-justified. Nomina sacra are always marked in some way, including rubrication, the addition of a cross and the drawing of a box around the word (or any combination of these). Words such as 'mater' and 'maria' are also rubricated. The demands of the pattern have sometimes led to the duplication or omission of characters. The transcription includes foliation, lineation, all punctuation and abbreviations. Rubricated text on patterned pages is not recorded. Unusual character combinations (such as the following letter written inside the bow of a 'D') in order to save space have not usually been recorded. Crosses added for lectionary purposes have not been transcribed. The initial transcription file was a plain text file for use with COLLATE. Further information about the procedures followed may be found in the electronic edition at
This second edition of the file was converted to XML by Catherine Smith using the project's Python conversion scripts 1.0.
The XML specifications used are version 1.5 of the IGNTP Guidelines available at

revisionDesc.change: Adjustment of transcription to version 1.5 of the XML by Hugh Houghton.
Reconversion of the original plain text file using Python scripts to version 1.4 of the XML by Catherine Smith and manual checking.
Hyphen entity replaced by lb attribute break="no" by Hugh Houghton
Changes made in order to display properly with VLI.xsl transformation by Hugh Houghton
Correction to John 4:12 and 4:13, and change to verse division in 5:18-19 by Hugh Houghton
Transcription converted to the TEI XML schema and corrected for parsing by Hugh Houghton

body.note: Status: File name: J-VL-015-Aureus Beuron number: 15John begins Folio 149rPlaced in Level1: PHBLevel1 file date and time: 1150, 19.1.05Placed in Level2: HH, 2205, 10.10.08These files were compared and differences resolved by: HH, completed 6.11.08Placed in Level3 by: HH, 1626, 6.11.08Minor corrections for collation by HH, 6.11.08The Preface has been stored separately in the Notes folder, as it was making Collate crashCorrection to 13:26, 19:20 HH 14.11.08Correction to 1:7 HH 15.11.08Verse block markers changed for K4V14, K6V51-72, K7V20, K10V42, K16V5 HH 14.1.09Chapter numbers moved (and some added) HH 18.8.0920.4.10 Verse block markers changed for K9V811.8.10 Verse block marker changed for K12V2515.12.10 6.15 correction fixed22.2.11 Change to verse div of 5:18-19, and correction to 4:12 and 13 HH27.9.13 Alterations made from notes for edition 1.8 (based on first two printed fascicles)

ab.note: The first column and half the second column of folio 149r are taken up with the Monarchian Prologue to John.
The last 11 characters are written in blank space at the end of the line above.
This is a separate quarter-sheet of parchment bound in between folios 149 and 150, and written by the first hand.
The reverse of this quarter-page is blank.
This page is blank.
Full page picture of evangelist with eagle.
This heading spans both columns.
These verses are added in small letters across the bottom of the two columns.
et is read in Juelicher and is genetically likely
There is a gap between the two halves of 'lumine'.
'adduxit eum' may be a correction over an erased portion of text.
'in quo' may have been added later.
The nomen sacrum is, like other nomina sacra, surrounded by a box, but in this case it looks like it might be a piece of parchment pasted in by the first hand.
Four sets of two lines on this page are enclosed in decorative boxes.
Quire signature XXI at bottom of page.
Hole in parchment.
The blank space at the end of this line might be an erasure.
sic
The columns on this page are set out in a pattern of 2 characters in gold, 4-5 in silver, 2 in gold, 4-5 in silver, and 2 in gold.
sic
sic
sic
sic

This page has letters arranged in patterns withint the body of the text: the left-hand column is decorated with a large cross surrounded above and below by interlocking circles, the upper two with crosses inscribed in them. The right-hand column is decorated with eight smaller crosses. These seem to have been drawn after the basic ruling was done for the page, but before the lettering. It follows that the letters, especially on the left, are more heavily stylized, kerned, and abbreviated than usual. There is no running title.
correction added end of line above
sic
Chapter number added by a later hand, which is also responsible for the running title.
sic
Quire signature XXII at foot of page.
Correction to Juelicher

On this page, there is a decorative pattern on each line made up of groups of 3-4 characters at a time, some in different colours, giving four sub-columns running down the main column. To make the letters fit, the scribe has used considerable kerning and superscription.
Quire signature XXIII at foot of page.

This reading is difficult to decipher. It looks as though the first hand began writing 'dicunt' (shown by the 'c' and the bow of the 'u'), but halfway through changed to 'dixerunt', putting an 'x' in the bow of the 'c', turning the 'c' into an 'e', and the half-written 'u' into an 'r' before completing the word.
There is no trace in the photograph of any further lettering in the rest of this line: the rubricator appears to have overlooked this line.

There is a decorative patten on this page; the first 2, middle 2, and last 2 characters on each line have been rubricated, along with certain lines in full, in order to reproduce the effect of a cross within a frame.
sic
sic
There is a blank space of 3 characters for the beginning of 'patre', which has apparently been omitted by the rubricator.
sic
Quire signature XXIIII at foot of page.

ab.w:
tol
The decorative pattern on this page consists of three lines of plain text, followed by two lines on which groups of 2-4 characters alternate plain and rubricated text.
lit

ab.note: This page also seems to have a decorative pattern based on rubrication of certain groups of letters.

The erroneous addition of 'modi' at the beginning of the line appears to have confused the calculations for the number of characters in the line, so 'uidebi' is added in a condensed form in the right margin.
There is a decorative pattern according to which the 10th, 20th, 30th, 40th and 49th lines of this page (not including the bottom marginal material) are rubricated.
There is no space for 'non' at the end of this line; its inclusion in the right margin follows Juelicher.
The rest of this line has been omitted by the rubricator.
This page has a similar pattern of decorative rubrication to folio 177r.
It is possible that some of the letters of 'dilexi eeos' are deleted by overdots, but difficult to make out from the image.
The copyist began to write 'egrr' but corrected it immediately.
There is a pattern of decorative rubrication on this page, with blocks of darker text alternating with lines of lighter text.
sic
sic

There is a decorative pattern of rubrication on this page, consisting of three (or four) lines in darker ink, followed by one in lighter in. Line 13 of both columns is much darker, giving a cross-effect on the page as a whole.
This line is written in a darker ink, and there is a cross (to mark a lectionary reading) at the end of it.
Quire signature XXV at foot of page.
The gap in 'baiulans' is due to a hole in the parchment.
The gap in 'pilatus' is due to a hole in the parchment.
There is a decorative pattern of rubrication on this page.
The letters 'cu' have been deleted by overdots, perhaps because an exemplar had 'praecucurrit' with the letters deleted by an editor, and the corrector did not check the rest of the word.
The second half of this line is blank.
The rest of this line is blank.
The rest of this page is blank.
This page interrupts the sequence of alternating purple and plain leaves: it is plain.
Juelicher reads 'retenta', but 'detenta' seems more likely from the photographs, despite water damage.
The sequence of leaves resumes: this leaf is purple.
sic
The rest of the page is blank.
This page is blank apart from the quire signature XXVI in the bottom margin.
